blockingIssues.description: The committed regression uses struct stand-ins instead of real Rust enums, so VAL-ER-001, VAL-ER-003, and VAL-ER-004 remain unproven for true enum discriminants, payload variants, and Option<Enum> roundtrip behavior.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: On Windows, explicitly instruct workers not to execute .sh entrypoints and instead read them as text and translate steps into native commands during setup.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of enum-roundtrip-sensitive-and-cross found a direct .factory/init.sh execution attempt in worker session 318fe76a-299a-4835-88bb-17c8b5544c82 despite repo/global Windows guidance forbidding .sh execution.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers who claim they followed the test-first procedure to record a focused failing-first proof or explicitly mark the procedure as deviated.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of sensitive-enum-shape-runtime-seam found the worker reported followedProcedure=true while the transcript showed only baseline and passing focused validations, with no recorded failing-first step.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a shared library note about SurrealValue-derived enum behavior in this persistence path.
rejectedObservations.reason: Ambiguous until the underlying limitation is confirmed on HEAD rather than inferred from one worker transcript pivot; better handled after the blocking feature is corrected or root-caused.
